2005-02-01.title: Iraqis Exercised Right to Vote and Paid With Their Lives
World Briefing | Europe: Vatican City: Catholics Rise Everywhere But Europe
2005-02-01.abstract: Vatican reports that number of Roman Catholics in world rose to 1.086 billion in 2003; Africa registered largest growth and only Europe declined in total number of Catholics (S)

2005-02-01.title: North Korea Is Said to Plan for Dynasty's Next Generation
2005-02-01.abstract: North Korean leader Kim Jong Il plans to stretch family dynasty into third generation, handing ocer power one day to one of his three sons;

2005-02-01.title: Europe to step up Iraq security effort
Besieged Chief of Atomic Agency Carries On
2005-02-01.abstract: Mohamed ElBaradei, director general of International Atomic Energy Agency, will address annual conference of World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, to flesh out his proposal for five-year moratorium on building uranium-enrichment facilities, to stop spread of atomic weapons to Iran or other countries; under his plan, countries would be able to lease enriched fuel from international consortium; this would enable them to operate nuclear reactors to generate electricity without having to build facilities that could be converted to weapons production (M)

2005-02-02.title: Iraqis Who Died While Daring to Vote Are Mourned as Martyrs
2005-02-02.abstract: Naim Rahim Yacoubi, one of at least 50 Iraqis who died in attacks on election day, is mourned in Najaf as Muslim martyr; photos; map; 37-year-old father of nine is buried with finger still ink-marked from voting, having died as he returned with tea for poll workers; dead include policemen who tried to stop suicide bombers and children walking with elderly parents to vote; Shiites who died are being brought to holy city for burials befitting their sacrifice; photos; map (M)

2005-02-02.title: China firm flouts edict to suspend dam work
Senator to Ask C.I.A. to Explain Nazi-File Stance
2005-02-02.abstract: Republican Sen Mike DeWine will demand public explanation from CIA Dir Porter Goss about agency's decision to withhold from government working group hundreds of thousands of pages of documents related to Nazi war criminals; group has broad legislative mandate to make public all such classified materials; dispute was dislosed by The New York Times; intelligence officials have said they do not believe they are required to disclose full details of all relationships with former Nazis (S)

2005-02-02.title: Sharon and Abbas Agree to Hold Summit Meeting in Egypt
Bill Clinton Chosen to Be U.N. Envoy for Tsunami Recovery
2005-02-02.abstract: UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan names Bill Clinton special envoy for tsunami-afflicted countries in southern Asia; Clinton says he will take up post after returning from visit to region with former Pres Bush; will also likely be asked to help settle longrunning disputes between governments and rebels in Indonesia and Sri Lanka (S)

2005-02-03.title: Police detain suspect in fatal hostage-taking
I.R.A. Says It Will Quit Peace Negotiations in Northern Ireland
2005-02-03.abstract: Irish Republican Army says it is withdrawing from peace negotiations in Northern Ireland and rescinding its proposals to disarm and cease paramilitary activities, because of what it calls false accusations and bad faith by British and Irish governments; does not say it would return to brutal violence that claimed most of 3,000 lives lost in 30-year conflict, or that it would end cease-fire that it says it has upheld since 1997; Prime Min Tony Blair of Britain and Prime Min Bertie Ahern of Ireland have blamed IRA for $50 million robbery from Belfast bank in Dec (M)

2005-02-03.title: France deals blow to EU services plan
Europe slow to dial its emergency number: 112
Photo shows fishermen missing since 1970s : The Koreas' past surfaces

2005-02-04.title: Europa: From America, Europe seems a long way off
U.S. pullout reflects new tsunami focus
Europeans seek role in training Iraq police
Inquiry on Food-for-Oil Plan Cites U.N. Diplomat for Conflict
2005-02-04.abstract: Commission investigating United Nations oil-for-food program in Iraq says in interim report that Benon V Sevan, former head of program, violated charter of UN and seriously undermined its integrity by helping company owned by friend to obtain valuable contracts to sell Iraqi oil; cites 'grave and continuing conflict of interest' in Sevan's conduct; report depicts what was UN's largest relief effort as riddled with political favoritism and mismanagement; program is also being investigated by five Congressional committees and federal prosecutor in New York; report says important parts of program were not audited, allowing evidence that Saddam Hussein demanded and got kickbacks from companies selling his oil to go undetected; report does not say Sevan benefited personally, but commission is still investigating $160,000 in cash that he received from aunt whose way of life does not suggest she was wealthy; senior UN official says Secretary General Kofi Annan will try to discipline Seven, who retired last year, and Joseph Stephanides, who oversaw selection of program's major contractors; photo of Paul A Volcker, who heads commission (M)

2005-02-05.title: Iraq seeks money back from relief program
World Briefing | Asia: Polio Cases Cut
2005-02-05.abstract: World Health Organization says Afghanistan, India and Pakistan are on target to wipe out polio by end of 2005 (S)

2005-02-05.title: World Briefing | Africa: Uganda: War Crimes Cases Advance
2005-02-05.abstract: Spokesman at International Criminal Court in Hague says arrest warrants are expected for up to dozen suspects linked to civil war in Uganda (S)

2005-02-06.title: U.S. Says 6 Americans Are Among Afghan Crash Dead
Sisters, Separated by the Holocaust, Reunite After 61 Years
2005-02-06.abstract: New computerized archive of Yad Vashem, Israel's center for Holocaust studies, reunites Klara Bleier and Hana Katz, sisters who were separated in Budapest ghetto in October 1944; sisters settled 45 miles apart in Israel in 1948, each thinking she was sole survivor of family; their mother died at Auschwitz, and they know nothing of fate of father or siblings; maps; photo (M)

2005-02-06.title: Mexico's Fallen Party Plans Its Revival With a New Star
2005-02-06.abstract: Roberto Madrazo Pintado, president of Mexico's Institutional Revolutionary Party (PRI), promotes reformed PRI that more than a third of Mexicans believe will recover presidency in 2006; PRI won plurality in Congress in 2003 and won 7 of 10 elections for governor in 2004; broad sense of disappointment in Pres Vicente Fox has left ample room for PRI comeback, but Madrazo's reputation is tarnished by accusations of fraud and betrayal, and analysts say he behaves like old school 'antidemocrat,' drawing power from desperate masses; photo; map (M)

2005-02-06.title: Two Fugitives From Russia at Bush Event
2005-02-06.abstract: Mikhail Brudno and Vladimir Dubov, Russian businessmen subject of international arrest warrants issued by Russia, have been granted long-term visas by United States and attended high-profile prayer breakfast with Pres Bush; Brudno and Dubov were investors in Group Menatap, investment company of oil company Yukos' founder, Mikhail B Khodorkovsky, and are charged with misappropriation of property and fraud, respectively; fled to Israel in 2004 and were granted citizenship (M)

2005-02-06.title: Rice Chides Russia on Quieting Dissent but Rejects Penalty
2005-02-06.abstract: Sec of State Condoleezza Rice tells Russian Foreign Min Sergey V Lavrov in Ankara, Turkey, that Pres Vladimir Putin's crackdown on dissent makes Russian-American relations 'more difficult,' but signals that United States will not try to isolate Russia, despite Pres Bush's inaugural vow to spread freedom; Rice emphasizes that cooperation with Russia has led to progress in combating terrorism; photo (M)

2005-02-07.title: C.I.A. Defers to Congress, Agreeing to Disclose Nazi Records
2005-02-07.abstract: Central Intelligence Agency bows to pressure from Congress and agrees to broad new interpretation of 1998 law that requires disclosure of classified records related to Nazi war criminals; Sen Mike DeWine calls this 'major breakthrough' in dispute waged in private for two years; had threatened to summon Central Intelligence Director Porter J Goss to testify in public on matter; CIA explicitly pledges for first time to 'acknowledge any relationship' between CIA and SS members, regardless of whether there was any information specifically tying them to war crimes (M)

2005-02-07.title: Pakistanis Go Fly a Kite as a Boisterous Rite of Spring
2005-02-07.abstract: Pakistanis enjoy boisterous annual rite of spring: kite-flying festival in Lahore; hundreds of thousands of kites soar and dip over city during Basant, Lahore's version of Carnival; photos; map (M)

2005-02-07.title: 6 From U.S. on Afghan Jet That Crashed
2005-02-07.abstract: United States Embassy in Kabul, Afghanistan, says initial indications are that 6 Americans were among 104 people on Kam Air plane that crashed Feb 3 in mountains southeast of Kabul; majority of passengers were Afghans (S)

2005-02-08.title: Nepal's King Cracks Down on Politics and News Media
2005-02-08.abstract: Nepalis are trying to adjust to combination of royal rule and martial law in week since King Gyanendra ended country's 15-year experiment with democracy and took power for himself; he imposed state of emergency and suspended host of civil liberties; those in politics and news media feel particularly under siege; Nepalis now have no freedom of assembly, expression or opinion; no right to information, property or privacy, and no protection from preventive detention; widespread international condemnation has not slowed arrest of political and student activists; king's stated reason for his actions is that country's fractious political parties had failed to hold elections or bring Maoist rebels to peace talks; photos; map (M)

2005-02-08.title: U.S. General to Help Guide Mideast Security
2005-02-08.abstract: Biographical sketch of Lt Gen William E Ward, appointed by Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice as security coordinator between Israelis and Palestinians; photo (M)

2005-02-08.title: World Briefing | Europe: Italy: Pope To Stay In Hospital
2005-02-08.abstract: Vatican spokesman Joaquin Navarro-Valls announces that Pope John Paul II will stay in hospital few more days as precautionary measure; Pope John Paul was admitted to Rome's Gemelli hospital with flu (S)

2005-02-08.title: Names of the Dead
2005-02-08.abstract: Defense Dept confirms deaths of two service members in Iraq (S)

2005-02-08.title: Web stylistas scoop up the news

2005-02-09.title: Thai villagers haunted by tsunami's missing
Skeptics question value of Bush budget cuts : Dollar's rise against euro may be tough to sustain
Drug Lord, Ruthless and Elusive, Reaches High in Mexico
2005-02-09.abstract: Arrest of aide to President Vicente Fox on suspicion of ties to drug traffickers suggests that criminal tentacles extend to highest levels of Mexican government; aide allegedly was in contact with syndicate of Joaquin Guzman Loera, clever ruthless gangster who has eluded agents again and again and ignited underworld war that has killed dozens of traffickers, police officers, federal agents and bystanders; photos; map; accused aide Nahum Acosta Lugo allegedly passed on details of Fox's travel plans to Guzman associates in Agua Prieta, which is part of drug lord's expanding turf (M)

2005-02-09.title: Over 1,000 Nepalese being held, opposition says
Give up nuclear program, Blair urges Iranian leaders

2005-02-10.title: Names of the Dead
2005-02-10.abstract: The Department of Defense has identified 1,446 American service members who have died since the start of the Iraq war. It confirmed the death of the following American yesterday:    BROCK, Sean L., 29, Capt., Marines; Redondo Beach, Calif.; Headquarters Battalion, Camp Butler, Okinawa.

2005-02-10.title: Recount of Disputed Votes to Delay Results
2005-02-10.abstract: A public recount of hundreds of disputed ballot boxes will delay the release of final results for Iraq's elections, election officials said Wednesday, leaving the shape of the nation's first democratically chosen government in doubt for at least a few more days.    The officials displayed a large stack of ballots and ballot boxes that had been ruled invalid. The striking display included boxes that appeared to have been hastily stuffed with forged ballots, as well as heaps of ballots in sacks and cartons that revealed an apparent attempt to falsify votes on a large scale.

2005-02-10.title: North Korea's Statement Puts China in a Quandary
NATO Members Pledge Support for Iraq Training Mission
A Woman? A Man? Whatever, a Star Athlete in Zimbabwe
2005-02-10.abstract: Zimbabwe's sports world was agog Wednesday over the revelation that one of the nation's leading female track stars, a winner of gold and silver medals in an array of events in Zimbabwe, Botswana and elsewhere, is in fact a man.     Or both man and woman. Or, so the track star says, a man now but once and maybe yet a woman.

2005-02-11.title: Suicide bomber kills 4 in Baghdad but misses U.S. convoy
World Briefing | Middle East: Iran: Revolution Celebrated With Threats
2005-02-11.abstract: Iranian Pres Mohammad Khatami, speaking to tens of thousands of demonstrators commemorating 26th anniversary of Islamic revolution in Tehran, says threat of United States invasion has no impact on Iranian people, who he holds will unite against all enemies; photo (S)

2005-02-11.title: Darfur's Babies of Rape Are on Trial From Birth
2005-02-11.abstract: Scores of babies are being born to girls and women who were raped as part of brutal battle over land and ethnicity in Darfur region of Sudan; fate of these children and their mothers is uncertain in society where deep taboos surround rape and identity is passed, according to Muslim tradition, from father to child; fact that these babies, and their mothers, are being referred to as janjaweed, insulting name given to Arab militiamen who terrorized region, underscores bitter division between those who identify themselves as Africans and those who see themselves as Arabs; it also points to potential difficulty of acceptance and integration in years ahead; for victims of rape, their children mark them forever and may spoil their chances at marriage or having normal life; for children, their future seems uncertain; Sudanese government foreign ministry spokesman has called accounts of rape in Darfur wildly exaggerated, but recent United Nations investigation into war crimes in Darfur lays out, in page after page, evidence of widespread and systematic rape in two-yaer conflict; photos; map (M)

2005-02-11.title: Gingerly, Saudis enter the world of elections

2005-02-12.title: World Briefing | Europe: Georgia: F.B.I Rules Premier's Death Was Accidental
2005-02-12.abstract: FBI says five agents helping Georgian officials investigate death of Prime Min Zurab Zhvania found no evidence of foul play and agreed that cause was accidental (S)

2005-02-12.title: France and Spain push EU charter
Japan Urges North Korea to Rejoin Disarmament Talks
2005-02-12.abstract: Japanese Prime Min Junichiro Koizumi urges North Korea to re-engage in disarmament talks day after it declares that it has nuclear weapons; says new law that will bar most of North Korea's ships from Japanese ports is not sanction; Vice Pres Dick Cheney presses South Korean Foreign Min Ban Ki Moon not to reward North by pursuing trade deals with country--point on which US and South Korea have been divided; photo (M)

2005-02-12.title: Rumsfeld drops in on Baghdad to gauge Iraqis

2005-02-13.title: Kurds Celebrate Their Strong Second-Place Showing
Israel Agrees to Free 500 Palestinian Prisoners
Brazil, Bowing to Protests, Reopens Logging in Amazon
2005-02-13.abstract: Brazilian government restores suspended logging licenses after loggers block major highway through heart of jungle and threaten violence; licenses were suspended last year in effort to impede deforestation in frontier areas of Amazon; dispute is latest of several in which government of Pres Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva has bowed to organized acts of civil disobedience; environmentalists express dismay; map; photos (M)

2005-02-13.title: Pope Greets Crowds at St. Peter's Square
Senator Clinton Plays It Cool at Munich Security Conference

2005-02-14.title: New leader of Togo defies criticism over deadly riots
Picking Up the Stolen Pieces of Iraq's Cultural Heritage
2005-02-14.abstract: Law enforcement officials and archaeologists say antiquities believed to have been looted from Iraq since American invasion include catalogued objects taken from museums and large but uncertain number of items that were pillaged from archaeological sites around country; photos (M)

2005-02-14.title: Abbas Declares War With Israel Effectively Over
2005-02-14.abstract: The Palestinian leader also said that Prime Minister Ariel Sharon is speaking "a different language" to the Palestinians.

2005-02-14.title: The Tsunami's Horror Haunts a Thai Fishing Village
2005-02-14.abstract: Foul lake of sludge is last hope for those in village of Nam Khem, Thailand, who have found no trace of missing family members since tsunami struck on Dec 26; almost half the population of 5,000 people are dead or missing; another 5,000 illegal immigrants from Myanmar are believed to have lived there, too; they are now gone, and nobody know what happened to them; none of survivors have returned from temporary shelters to live in ruins; photo; map (M)

2005-02-14.title: In Munich, Senator Clinton Urges NATO Role in Sudan Conflict
2005-02-14.abstract: Sen Hillary Rodham Clinton, addressing annual Munich Conference on Security Policy, advocates direct NATO role to stop killing in Darfur region of Sudan, including logistical, communication and transportation support; her speech is collaborative effort; she sought input from, among others, former Clinton administration officials Richard C Holbrooke and Samuel R Berger (M)

2005-02-15.title: Seoul says it doubts claim by North on nuclear arms
Bush Seeks $81.9 Billion More, Mostly for Forces in Iraq
2005-02-15.abstract: President Bush sent to Congress on Monday a request for $81.9 billion in additional spending to cover the costs of military and intelligence operations in Iraq and Afghanistan, tsunami relief in Asia, revamping the Army, and new death benefits for families of service members killed in combat.     The White House announced the broad outlines of its request for this year nearly three weeks ago, saying about $75 billion would go to military activities, mostly for the Army. The rest would be devoted to reconstruction costs, mostly in Afghanistan and Iraq, and the activities of the State Department and other agencies, including the new Director of National Intelligence.

2005-02-15.title: Fire Kills 59 and Injures 200 in Crowded Mosque in Tehran
2005-02-15.abstract: Fire broke out Monday evening in a crowded mosque in central Tehran, killing 59 people and injuring more than 200, the news agency ISNA reported.     The fire was ignited by an oil heater that had been taken to the women's section of the mosque to combat the bitingly cold weather that has gripped Iran for more than a week.

2005-02-15.title: Israel is at crossroads of peace, says Sharon

2005-02-16.title: Iraq Holy City Suffers Lack of Utilities and Pilgrims
2005-02-16.abstract: Najaf, holy Shiite city once thronged with pilgrims, is empty of visitors, with entire blocks of Old City still in ruins from battle between US forces and Shiite militia nearly six months ago; photo; Americans make brief forays into markets and hold up city as model of stability for Iraq, but it is mired in economic problems (M)

2005-02-16.title: Senators Criticize U.N.'s Ban on Testimony in Iraqi Oil Inquiry
2005-02-16.abstract: Leaders of Senate investigations subcommittee criticize UN for not providing access to its officials and documents for probe of oil-for-food program in Iraq; chairman Norm Coleman and Democrat Carl Levin complain that UN auditor Dileep Nair has not been allowed to testify and call on Annan to lift diplomatic immunity from Benon V Sevan, who ran program; officials of contractors Saybolt and Cotecna testify; Cotecna head Robert M Massey says UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan's son Kojo had no role in getting contract (M)

2005-02-16.title: Gaza Pullout Plan Advances, Even as 2 of Its Architects Depart
Rice Sees Tensions With Syria Growing After Assassination

2005-02-17.title: Iraq to Announce Makeup of New Assembly
Mexico City's Mayor, Facing Arrest, Is Taking His Case to Streets
2005-02-17.abstract: Mexico City Mayor Andres Manuel Lopez Obrador says he will start his presidential campaign from jail, if forced to, as Mexican Congress moves closer to vote that could lead to his arrest and prosecution on obscure contempt charges in connection to construction of hospital access road; polls consistently show Lopez as leading contender to succeed Pres Vicente Fox; Fox has said case against Lopez proves no public officials are above law, but Lopez questions Fox's motives (M)

2005-02-17.title: He's in the spotlight again, but there is little illumination
Iran Says Pilotless U.S. Jets Are Spying on Nuclear Sites
2005-02-17.abstract: Iran says American pilotless spy planes have been seen over its nuclear sites; threatens to shoot them down if they come within range; White House refuses commen; issue adds to rising concern in Iran over possible American surveillance operations in country (M)

2005-02-17.title: Human Rights Commissioner Urges U.N. to Act Quickly on Darfur
2005-02-17.abstract: United Nations' top human rights official Louise Arbour says any delay in prosecuting war crimes from Darfur region of Sudan would risk lives and end hope of forging eventual peace; statement intensifies pressure on United States to put aside its opposition to International Criminal Court; UN human rights commission has given Secretary Gen Kofi Annan list of 51 names of suspects and evidence against them (M)

2005-02-18.title: Irish Police Raids Seek Link Between Robbery and I.R.A.
AIDS Blamed as South Africa's Mortality Rate Soars
Muslim region adopts a 'new' language: Bosnian
China Is Uneasy in Korean Role, Wary of U.S. Motives
World Briefing | Asia: Nepal: Calls To Block Military Aid
2005-02-18.abstract: Amnesty International's secretary general Irene Khan says she will declare imminent emergency in Nepal if basic human rights are not restored by King Gyanendra; calls on primary donors to suspend military assistance; James F Moriarty, recalled American ambassador to Nepal, echos Khan's sentiments and threatens to withdraw or diminish American military aid, which is budgeted at $1.5 million this year (S)

2005-02-19.title: Suicide Bombers Kill at Least 35 in Baghdad Area
2005-02-19.abstract: Five suicide bombers, some disguised in robes of religious pilgrims, explode their bombs in crowds of worshipers and at police check point in Baghdad area, killing at least 35 people on eve of Shiite holy day of Ashura; explosions raise fears of repeat of last year's carnage, when about 180 people were killed during Ashura celebrations; photos (M)

2005-02-19.title: World Briefing | Africa: Zambia: Rabid Dogs On Loose
2005-02-19.abstract: Rabid dogs reportedly have bitten over 100 people in northern Zambia, mostly in areas struck by floods and inaccessible to vehicles (S)

2005-02-19.title: Spain likely to vote for EU constitution
U.N. Official Rejects Calls to Step Down Under Fire
2005-02-19.abstract: UN High Commissioner for Refugees Ruud Lubbers refuses to step down over sexual harassment charges; Lubbers emerges from talks with Sec Gen Kofi Annan and denies that his resignation had been discussed; dismisses his accusers as liars and attacks UN for slandering him; Lubbers photo (M)

2005-02-19.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Lebanon: Cabinet Minister Quits
2005-02-19.abstract: Lebanon Tourism Min Farid Khazen resigns following assassination of former Prime Min Rafiq Hariri (S)

2005-02-20.title: Israel Gears Up for Burst of Far-Right Anger
2005-02-20.abstract: Far-right Israelis grow increasingly strident in lead-up to planned withdrawal of Israeli settlers from Gaza Strip this summer; many people draw parallel to period preceding assassination of Prime Min Yitzhak Rabin in 1995 and fear for Prime Min Ariel Sharon's safety; some senior officials call for pre-emptive steps like administrative detention without trial, legal step rarely used against Israelis; protests assume aggressive edge, but leaders of organized opposition in Gaza and West Bank say they reject violence; photo (M)

2005-02-20.title: China Denounces United States-Japan Agreement on Taiwan
Testing New Ban, Britons Run With the Hounds
2005-02-20.abstract: Hundreds of people gather in Great Britain on horseback and foot to ride and run with hounds in defiance of new law forbidding hunting with intent to kill foxes and allowing hounds to do killing; hunters insist they will test law without actually breaking it, under legal guise of exercising hounds; some see situation as crisis of confidence in government; others say fight is urban-rural battle or class warfare; photos (M)

2005-02-20.title: Suicide Bombers Aim at a Shiite Holy Day in Iraq
2005-02-20.abstract: Six suicide bombers attack crowds in Iraq celebrating Shiite holiday Ashura, killing as many as 39 people and injuring about 150 others; group led by Abu Musab al-Zarqawi claims responsibility for suicide bombing outside Iraqi Army building in Baquba; American soldier is killed in attacks near Kadimain shrine; bomber on bicycle rides into tent of Sunni mourners at funeral in Baghdad, mistaking them for Shiites; photos (M)

2005-02-20.title: Internal Tension Simmers in Kuwait as Islamic Politicians Clash With Pro-Westerner Liberals
2005-02-20.abstract: New threat of terrorism from within exposes tension between Islamic politicians and pro-Western liberals in Kuwait; crisis began on Jan 10 when police uncovered suspected Al Qaeda cell planning attacks on American troops and various sites; gun battles followed on Jan 30 and Jan 31; analysts say government was caught off-guard at critical moment, as Emir Sheikh Jaber al-Ahmad al-Sabah and Crown Prince Sheik Saad al-Abdullah al-Sabah are both ill and power vacuum looms; photo (M)

2005-02-21.title: Names of the Dead
2005-02-21.abstract: Defense Department confirms deaths of two service members in Iraq (S)

2005-02-21.title: Ex-presidents take close look at tsunami damage
Bush warns Russia on the rule of law
Who Needs the N.H.L. When the Moose Is on the Loose?
2005-02-21.abstract: Winnipeg's American Hockey League team, high-scoring Manitoba Moose, emerges as hottest box office draw of any professional hockey team in Canada; city has not had NHL game since 1996, when Winnipeg Jets left for Phoenix; photos; map (M)

2005-02-22.title: New Course by Royal Navy: A Campaign to Recruit Gays
2005-02-22.abstract: Royal Navy begins actively encouraging gays to enlist and pledges to make life easier when they do, five years after Britain lifted its ban on gays in military; navy says it has asked Stonewall, group that lobbies for gay rights, to help it develop better strategies for recruiting and retaining gay men and lesbians, possibly by advertising in gay mgazines and newspapers; new effort continues pattern of changing official attitudes in navy dating to 1999, when European court ruled that Britain's ban on gays in military violated European human-rights laws; gays in British miltary are subject to same rules of sexual conduct as heterosexuals: no touching, no kissing, no flaunting of sexuality; despite change in policy, relatively few gay men and lesbians already in military have come out (M)

2005-02-22.title: Afghan Living Standards Among the Lowest, U.N. Finds
2005-02-22.abstract: Afghanistan is well behind its neighbors in several indicators of poverty and vulnerability in latest United Nations Human Development Index; report warns that country could revert to anarchy if its dire poverty, poor health and insecurity are not improved; report notes that despite its massive problems, Afghanistan has shown remarkable progress in three years since United States-led war in 2001 drove out Taliban; photo; chart (M)

2005-02-22.title: Syria Pledges Pullout Again Amid Protests by Lebanese
2005-02-22.abstract: Syrian Pres Bashar al-Assad repeats longstanding promise to withdraw his troops from Lebanon, as tens of thousands of people march through streets of Beirut in largest anti-Syrian protests since killing of former Prime Min Rafik Hariri week ago; political analysts dismiss Assad's pledge, made to Arab League secretary general Amr Moussa, as mere wordplay; hours after Moussa reports on pledge, Syrian government seeks to minimize its effects; opposition figures say Syrian control over Lebanon is so tight that it can easily make promises without fear of ever having to make good on them; photo (M)

2005-02-23.title: Nicaragua Lags on Vow to Scrap Portable Missiles
2005-02-23.abstract: State Department and Pentagon officials express renewed concern that Nicaragua may not destroy its arsenal of portable antiaircraft missiles as it promised during Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld's visit to Managua in 2004; American delegation is in Managua to renew pressure on Nicaraguan government to destroy all of shoulder-fired missiles supplied by Cuba and Soviet Union during 1980's (M)

2005-02-23.title: Iceland to Grant Passport to Fischer
British soldiers guilty of Iraq abuse
ETA has deep pockets, police official says

2005-02-24.title: Canada Says It Won't Join Missile Shield With the U.S.
2005-02-24.abstract: Canadian government refuses to take part in planned North America missile defense system despite personal lobbying by Pres Bush there in Nov; long-awaited decision from Prime Min Paul Martin is symbolic setback for Bush administration as it tries to heal rifts with allies emerging from invasion of Iraq; administration officials say Canada's decision will not deter United States; Martin had supported Canada's participation in missile defense program before he became Liberal Party leader in 2003; he reversed his position amid strong opposition from backbench Liberal Party members in House of Commons, and polls showing system to be unpopular with public (M)

2005-02-24.title: Karzai visits India to bolster economic ties
In Belarus and Moldova, hopes for democracy
2 British Soldiers Convicted of Abusing Prisoners in Iraq
2005-02-24.abstract: Two British soldiers are convicted of abusing Iraqi detainees by military jury in Germany in case widely known as Britain's Abu Ghraib; third soldier has pleaded guilty; incidents all took place in May 2003 during operation to stop looting at aid depot near Basra; abuse came to light after one soldier, having returned home, dropped off roll of photographs at shop showing naked Iraqi detainees in humiliating sexual poses (M)

2005-02-25.title: World Briefing | United Nations: Oil-For-Food Figure Seeks Extension
2005-02-25.abstract: Benon V Sevan, former head of United Nations Oil-for-Food program, asks for extention to respond to charges filed two weeks ago that claim he helped friend obtain contracts through program; Fred Eckhard, spokesman for United Nations Sec Gen Kofi Annan, says request is being considered (S)

2005-02-25.title: World Briefing | Americas: Mexico: Jet Crash Kills Governor
2005-02-25.abstract: Colima, Mexico, Governor Gustavo Vazquez Montes, three aides and two pilots are killed when state-owned jet crashes into mountains near Tzitzio town; cause of crash is unknown (S)

2005-02-25.title: Bush and Putin on Democracy, Political Debate and Iraq
2005-02-25.abstract: Excerpts from transcript of joint news conference of Pres Bush and Pres Vladimir V Putin of Russia in Bratislava, Slovakia (M)

2005-02-25.title: Bishops in Croatia assail fertility method
Military policy on gays costs U.S. $200 million

2005-02-26.title: Pope Described As Recovering After Surgery
2005-02-26.abstract: Vatican says Pope John Paul II appears to be recovering from tracheotomy; denies reports that pope had been attached to ventilator, and says procedure was elective; medical experts say tracheotomy poses new dangers for pope; his health has been fragile for years, but question of his ability to lead church has been taboo; this second hospitalization is forcing Vatican officials and Roman Catholics around world to come to grips with what may be much-changed papacy; photos (M)

2005-02-26.title: Togo Ruler, Surrendering to Pressure, Steps Down
2005-02-26.abstract: Faure Gnassingbe bows to international pressure and will step down three weeks after military installed him to succeed his late father, Gnassingbe Eyadema, as Togo's president; he will be replaced by Abass Bonfo, member of Parliament, who will become interim president and oversee elections within 60 days (M)

2005-02-26.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Egypt: DNA Test Ordered In Paternity Case
2005-02-26.abstract: Judge in Cairo's Family Court orders DNA test to settle unprecedented paternity suit that has riveted Egyptians for months; Hind el-Hinnawy has sued actor Ahmed el-Fishawy, saying he fathered her four-month-old girl during their secret marriage (S)

2005-02-26.title: African Union demands that Togo's army-installed leader resign
Flaws Reported in Screening Subcontractors for Iraq Prisons
2005-02-26.abstract: Report by Justice Dept Inspector Gen Glenn A Fine says that dozens of subcontractors hired in 2003 to help Iraq rebuild its prison system did not undergo thorough background checks into allegations of misconduct by some of them at US prisons; concludes that none of their backgrounds would have disqualified them from working in Iraq and that none of them were involved in abuses at Abu Ghraib prison; report was prompted by request for investigation from Sen Charles E Schumer (M)

2005-02-27.title: Kurds Vow to Retain Militia as Guardians of Autonomy
2005-02-27.abstract: Kurdish leaders say they will write into new Iraqi Constitution system granting considerable powers to individual regions and legitimizing their use of militia called pesh merga; about 100,000 pesh merga would nominally fall under Defense Ministry in Baghdad, but really be controlled by regional commanders; major Shiite parties may try to block Kurdish autonomy; American commanders argue that all militias must be state-sponsored and not organized by ethnicity or sect; photo (M)

2005-02-27.title: Within C.I.A., Worry of Prosecution for Conduct
2005-02-27.abstract: Career officers at Central Intelligence Agency fear prosecution and punishment for conduct during interrogations and detentions of terrorism suspects; several cases are currently under review by Justice Dept; concern in ranks has been growing since removal of Baghdad station chief in December 2003 because of concerns about deaths of two Iraqis questioned by CIA employees (M)

2005-02-27.title: Crowds at St. Peter's Are on Tours, Not a Papal Death Watch
2005-02-27.abstract: In wake of Pope John Paul II's tracheotomy, after second trip in one month to hospital, there is sense in Rome that significant corner has been turned in his long public illness, but it has not yet manifested itself openly as vigil; Pope will not take part in today's weekly public Angelus blessing and prayer for what seems to be first time in 26 years, but he reportedly is otherwise in good condition; photo (M)

2005-02-27.title: Mubarak Pushes Egypt to Allow Freer Elections
2005-02-27.abstract: Egyptian Pres Hosni Mubarak asks Parliament to amend Constitution to allow direct, multiparty presidential elections for first time; some opposition politicians and analysts hail proposal, while skeptics await details; proponents regard move as first step in reviewing entire Constitution and response to domestic Bush administration pressure; Egypt's Parliament has long history of diluting reforms; recent arrest of Ayman Nour, leader of newly formed opposition party, seems at odds with Mubarak's commitment to democracy; photos (M)

2005-02-27.title: Islamic Jihad Says It WasBehind Tel Aviv Bombing
2005-02-27.abstract: Islamic Jihad claims responsibility for suicide bombing in Tel Aviv; says one-month pause in attacks is over and will not be extended because Israel has continued to arrest and kill Palestinians; Israel blames Syria for supporting Islamic Jihad, and Israeli and Palestinian security forces each make arrests in West Bank; Palestinian Pres Mahmoud Abbas, speaking before Islamic Jihad's claim, explicitly condemns bombing (M)

2005-02-28.title: Europe Union Upsets Croatia With Demands
2005-02-28.abstract: Croatian Foreign Min Kolinda Grabar-Kitarovic says European Union is making impossible demand in requiring that war crimes suspect be turned over to international tribunal at The Hague as condition for starting negotiations on membership; contends that there is no evidence that Gen Ante Gotovina is in Croatia, although tribunal says he is; Western diplomats say Croatia is not doing everything it can to comply (M)

2005-02-28.title: A Qualified Welcome for Mubarak Proposal
2005-02-28.abstract: Pres Hosni Mubarak's unexpected announcement that Egypt will hold its first direct, multiparty presidential election is seen by commentators as important, if small, step on road to reform; they expect no sweepingg change from Mubarak, noting he has spent 24 years making sure nothing ruffles political life in Egypt (M)

2005-02-28.title: Talks begin to end Iraq weapons search
